revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:出雲振根]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|relatives={{ill|Iiirine|qid=Q55533680|1=Iiirine}}}}
{{Infobox noble | name = Izumo Furune | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = [[Kofun period]] | birth_date = Unknown | death_date = After the 60th year of Emperor Sujin's reign, 7th month of autumn | other_names = | posthumous_name = | divine_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = | Rank = | lord = | clan = | parents = | siblings = | children = | notable_works = }}
'''Izumo Furune''' (出雲振根, [[dates of birth and death unknown]]) was a local noble from the [[Kofun period]] mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}. There is no reference to him in the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|qid=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}, but a similar account is given in which {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|倭建命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|1=Yamato Takeru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q461258}} defeated {{ill|Izumo Takeru|ja|出雲建|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Takeru|1=Izumo Takeru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q28688775}}.
== Overview == According to the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}, Emperor {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|qid=Q333530|lt=Sujin|1=Emperor Sujin}} decreed to his retainers, "I wish to see the divine treasures said to have been brought from heaven by {{ill|Takehinatori|ja|武日照命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehinatori|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 建比良鳥命|1=Takehi-Nateru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11065428}}, also called Takehinatori or Amenohinatori. These treasures are stored in the shrine of Izumo Ōkami ({{ill|Kumano Taisha|en|Kumano Taisha|qid=Q11568887|lt=Kumano Taisha|1=Kumano Taisha}} or {{ill|Izumo-taisha|lt=Izumo-taisha|lt=Izumo Taisha|ja|杵築神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Taisha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}})." To fulfill this wish, a warrior named {{ill|Take Morosumi|ja|矢田部造武諸隅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Take Morosumi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (also recorded as Ōmorosumi), an ancestor of the Yatabe clan, was dispatched to present the treasures.
At that time, the treasures were managed by {{ill|Izumo Furune|ja|出雲振根|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Furune|qq=}}, an ancestor of the Izumo clan, but he was away in {{ill|Tsukushi Province|en|Tsukushi Province|qid=Q1206461|lt=Tsukushi Province|1=Tsukushi Province}}. In his absence, his brother {{ill|Iiinone|ja|飯入根|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iiinone|1=Iiirine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q55533680}}, acting independently, accepted the imperial command and sent the treasures to the court with his brother {{ill|Umashikara Hisa|ja|甘美韓日狭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umashikara Hisa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and son {{ill|Ukazukunu|ja|鸕濡渟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukazukunu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
Upon his return from Tsukushi, Furune learned of this and reproached his brother:
*"You should have waited a few days. Why did you so easily hand over the divine treasures?"*
Unable to let go of his resentment, Furune harbored a grudge for years and eventually decided to kill his brother. He deceived Iiinone by saying:
*"Lately, reeds have grown thick around the pools of Stopoya (modern-day {{ill|Izumo, Shimane|en|Izumo, Shimane|qid=Q825166|lt=Izumo City|1=Izumo, Shimane}}, Shimane Prefecture). Let’s go and see them together."*
At the poolside, Furune said:
*"The water is so clear. Why don’t we bathe together?"*
Furune then tricked his brother by swapping his sword with a wooden replica he had prepared earlier. As Iiinone emerged from the water, Furune killed him with the real sword.
This scene was immortalized in a waka:
{{quotation|Yagumo rises, proud warrior of Izumo, with a blade bound in black ivy, alas, no true blade to be found.}}
Iiinone's brother and son reported the details to the imperial court. As a result, Emperor Sujin dispatched generals {{ill|Kibitsuhiko|ja|吉備津彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibitsuhiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉備津彦命|1=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q65318}} and {{ill|Takenu Nakawake|ja|武渟川別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenu Nakawake|1=Take Nunokawa Wake no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11546050}} to kill Furune.
Subsequently, the Izumo clan refrained from worshiping Izumo Ōkami until a woman named {{ill|Hikato-be|ja|氷香戸辺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikato-be|1=hikatobe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q55533080}} from {{ill|Tanba Province|en|Tanba Province|qid=Q954511|lt=Tanba Province|1=Tanba Province}}, [[Hikami District]], sang a song taught to her child. This led the emperor to reinstate the worship of Izumo Ōkami with mirrors.<ref>Nihon Shoki, Emperor Sujin's 60th year, 7th month, 14th day.</ref>
Eight years later, Emperor Sujin passed away.
According to the Nihon Shoki, 34 years later, in the 26th year of Emperor {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|qid=Q314850|lt=Suinin|1=Emperor Suinin}}, the imperial court sent the high-Ranking official {{ill|Mononobe no Tōchine|ja|物部十千根|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mononobe no Tōchine|1=Mononobe no Tokone|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q24885356}} to inspect the divine treasures in Izumo.<ref>Nihon Shoki, Emperor Suinin's 26th year, 8th month, 3rd day.</ref>
== Analysis == The above story closely resembles the tactics employed by {{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q461258}} in defeating Izumo Takeru during Emperor {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|qid=Q329723|lt=Keikō|1=Emperor Keikō}}'s reign, suggesting a conflation of traditions. Notably, the Nihon Shoki's account of Yamato Takeru does not mention any visit to Izumo.
Many theories identify the "Izumo Ōkami" referenced in the treasure-offering episode with {{ill|Izumo-taisha|lt=Izumo-taisha|lt=Izumo Taisha|ja|杵築大社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Taisha|qq=}}, but since the treasures were associated with the ancestors of the Izumo clan, there is also a possibility of {{ill|Kumano Taisha|en|Kumano Taisha|qid=Q11568887|lt=Kumano Taisha|1=Kumano Taisha}} in {{ill|Ou District|ja|意宇郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ou District|1=Ou district|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11073594}}. Scholars such as {{ill|Mitsusada Inoue|ja|井上光貞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitsusada Inoue|1=Mitsusada Inoue|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11372857}} have argued that the narrative reflects a rivalry between eastern Izumo groups worshiping Kumano Taisha and western Izumo groups worshiping Izumo Taisha.<ref>Nihon Shoki (Vol. 1), Iwanami Shoten, 1994, p. 299.</ref>
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== References == * {{ill|Concise Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja|コンサイス日本人名辞典 改訂新版|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Concise Japanese Biographical Dictionary|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, p. 105 (Sanseido, 1993) * {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|qid=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}, Complete Annotated Edition, Shogakukan, 1983 * {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}, Volume 1, Iwanami Bunko, 1994 * {{ill|Complete Modern Translations of Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀全現代語訳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Complete Modern Translations of Nihon Shoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Vol. 1, Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko, translated by Takeshi Ujiyasu, 1988 * {{ill|History of Japan 1: From Myth to History|ja|日本の歴史1 神話から歴史へ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/History of Japan 1: From Myth to History|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, by Mitsusada Inoue, Chūōkōronsha, 1965
== See Also == * {{ill|Imperial Regalia of Japan|en|Imperial Regalia of Japan|qid=Q327465|lt=Three Sacred Treasures|1=Imperial Regalia of Japan}} * {{ill|Amenohiboko|en|Amenohiboko|qid=Q713100|lt=Amenohiboko|1=Amenohiboko}} * {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|qid=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru}} * {{ill|Izumo Takeru|ja|出雲建|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Takeru|1=Izumo Takeru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q28688775}} * {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|qid=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin|1=Emperor Sujin}} * {{ill|Hikato-be|ja|氷香戸辺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikato-be|1=hikatobe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q55533080}} * {{ill|Aoyakamijichi Site|en|Aoyakamijichi Site|qid=Q17988246|lt=Aotani Kamijiji Ruins|1=Aoyakamijichi Site}} * {{ill|List of Pre-Asuka Period People|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Pre-Asuka Period People|1=list of ancient Japanese figures|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11665973}}
